person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
mort à Tripoli Liban Guillaume-Hugues DES BAUX (1055-1105), Seigneur des Baux et de Marignane et chevalier croisé (1096-1099). Fin octobre 1095, il est dans les rangs de l'armée des Provençaux avec Raymond de Saint-Gilles. Sa femme Vierne de Posquières et ses fils Raimond et Guillaume se croisent aussi. Ils auraient été 100.000 à passer les Alpes au Mont Genèvre. Ils traversent l'Italie du Nord, la Dalmatie, l'Albanie, coupent par la Macédoine pour arriver en Thrace en avril 1097, puis le 22 avril à Constantinople. A Constantinople, ils rejoignent les Croisés de Godefroy de Bouillon et de Bohémon. Ces derniers prennent Nicée le 6 mai 1097. En juillet 1097, ils prennent Dorylée, le 27 septembre 1097, ils sont devant Césarée puis, ils font le siège d'Antioche durant sept mois, le 21 octobre. En mai 1099, ils prennent Tripoli, Beyrouth, Saïda, Tyr, et arrivent à Jérusalem le 7 juin. Ils assiègent cette ville le 15 juillet. Donation: 1091 à Arles,13 004,Bouches-du-Rhône,Provence-Alpes-Côte-d'Azur,France
relevé JPR Teyssier, Chalancon, Mangas, Verger, Lecomte

zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume Hugues des Baux於大約1047年出生在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o萊斯 包-德-普洛福斯時，他的父親Hugues I des Baux67歲，母親Inauris Agoult de Reillane19歲。他於大約1071年與Vierne DE POSQUIERES在法國布-度-o萊斯 包-德-普洛福斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1105年1月1日在利比亞t阿拉布盧斯的黎波里享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: des
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Alsace and Lorraine): from a shortened form of the German personal name   Debus  .

variants.name: Debus
meanings: German: from a personal name, a vernacular short form of Matthäus (see Matthew ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine); compare Debs .
French: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone from any of various places called Bus (from a regional form of bois ‘wood’) in Eure, Pas-de-Calais, and Somme.

nameMeanings.name: Baux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Indian (mainly West Bengal) and Bangladeshi: from Bengali baul, Sanskrit vatula ‘madman’. Bearers of this name traditionally lived a lifestyle characterized by isolation from society, typically singing of social inequalities and injustice. They subsisted mainly on alms, considering themselves to be outside any organized religion, representing instead a polymorphic spiritual tradition.
German: relationship name from a term denoting a relative or close friend, from Middle High German buole, Middle Low German bole ‘relative, lover, close friend’.
German: possibly also from a rare variant of the personal name   Paul  .

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Hugues
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hugues Capet ( ?938–96 ) was ruler of France ( 987–96 ) and founder of the Capetian dynasty. See Hugh .

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume Hugues des Baux was born about 1047, in Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, his father, Hugues I des Baux, was 67 and his mother, Inauris Agoult de Reillane, was 19. He married Vierne DE POSQUIERES about 1071, in Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1 January 1105, in Tripoli, Ţarābulus, Libya, at the age of 59.

Il a épousé Vierne DE POSQUIERES environ 1071, à Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume Hugues des Bauxが約1047年にフランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンレス バウクス-デ-プローベンスで生まれた時，父Hugues I des Bauxは67歳，母Inauris Agoult de Reillaneは19歳であった。約1071年にフランスボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンレス バウクス-デ-プローベンスでVierne DE POSQUIERESと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1105年1月1日にリビアtアルaブルストリポリで59歳で死去した。

ko.Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône,: 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스
ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume Hugues des Baux(이)가 대략 1047년 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugues I des Baux(은)는 67세였고, 그의 어머니 Inauris Agoult de Reillane(은)는 19세였다. 그는 대략 1071년 프랑스 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스에서 Vierne DE POSQUIERES(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1105년 1월 1일 리비아 t아a버루스 트리폴리에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume Hugues des Baux родился около 1047 г. в местности лесь баукс-де-провенс, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция, его отцу по имени Hugues I des Baux было 67 лет, а матери, которую звали Inauris Agoult de Reillane, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Vierne DE POSQUIERES около 1071 г. в местности лесь баукс-де-провенс, боучес-ду-рoн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1 января 1105 г. в местности триполи, tарaбулус, Ливия в возрасте 59 лет.
